titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 234
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

msDesc.head: Miscellany with moral and theological works

p.note: In black ink by a nineteenth-century hand: Schürer von Waldheim. Kat. no 105.

musicNotation.p: Five staffs of Hufnagel notation for the Alleluia-verse O consolatrix pauperum Maria.

binding.p: Late medieval binding. Brown leather over wooden boards. 3 double raised bands, 2 single bands. 2 catchplates for hooked clasps on . On , two paper labels: on the upper one traces of an old signum, the lower one is the National Library book label with the current signum in ink. Parchment pastedown on with a mass formulary for the conception of Mary with the Alleluia verse O consolatrix pauperum and Hufnagelnotation.

decoNote.p: 3-line blind tooling. On : a frame filled with a diamond pattern with a small five-petal flower where the lines cross. On : a frame filled with a vertical and a diagonal cross.

binding.condition: Leather is scratched and worn, board showing at places on . Leather is cracked between the covers and the , leaving the cord joints between the spine and the covers visible. The three lower cords joining the spine to the are broken. Clasps and leather straps are missing. Remnants of straps are still attached to the anchorplates on the .

origin.p: Germany Lüneburg Wolfsburg Castle , St Michael's monastery, written between 1474-1489, as attested to in the colophons on (1474), (1475), (1477), (1488), (1488), (1489).

provenance.p: In the 19th century the manuscript belonged to Schürer von Waldheim.

acquisition.p: Purchased by the National Library in September from Schürer von Waldheim. Listed in the catalogue as no. 105 and described as Miscellanea på Latin och Neder Tyska.

msItem.title: Disticha Catonis
msItem.incipit: SI deus est animus vt nobis carmina dicunt
msItem.explicit: hoc breuitas fecit sensus coniungere binis
msItem.note:
Two additions below the text on by two different, contemporary or near-contemporary hands. The first one consists of four hexameter lines: Si vis seruare pacem simul hec tria serua / Non des consilium de quo te nemo requirit / Non inuestiges que nec te nec tua tangunt / Non rege que tibi non fuerunt commissa regenda / Ex historia troiana. The second one is an elegiac distich: Hoc scio pro recto si contra stercora certo / Vinco uel vincor semper ego maculor, listed in Walther (1964)
.

msItem.title: Facetus
msItem.incipit: CVm nichil vtilius humane credo saluti
msItem.explicit: Femina casta bonus socius fidelis amicus
msItem.finalRubric: Et sic est finis liber faceti
msItem.note: The order and number of the verses in the manuscript differ from the edited texts.

msItem.title: Regimen scholarium
msItem.incipit: Scholarius si vis prouehi culmen ad honoris
msItem.explicit: Quas tu si seruaueris te honore reges
msItem.note:
The rubric is written in the outer column. Listed in Walther (1959)
.

msItem.title: Peniteas cito
msItem.incipit: PEniteas cito peccator cum sit miserator
msItem.explicit: Cum de corde reo sunt criminaque fugata
msItem.note:
Additions in German and Latin on . Listed in Walther (1959)
, and Bloomfield et al. (1979)
.

bibl.title: PL 207
bibl.citedRange: (The second half of the poem differs from the version edited in PL)

msItem.title: Poeta salutaris with glosses and commentary
msItem.incipit: AVrea lux oritur pratum pete collige flores
msItem.explicit: Floret huius honor carminis atque decor
msItem.note:
The glosses and comments, in Latin and German, end after . Listed in Walther (1959)
.

msItem.title: Doctrinale minus (Liber parabolarum) with commentary
msItem.rubric: INcipit liber naturalissimi poete magistri alani In prouerbiis
msItem.incipit: A phebo phebe lumen capit
msItem.explicit: Seperari a te mors perpetua Adherere tibi vita eterna Ad quam nos perducat Qui viuit et regnat in secula seculorum Amen

note.p: The title (corrected) and the two first lines of the poem are written on . Two additions have been made in the space below, originally left blank. The first addition on concerns the invocation annanisapta (sometimes spelled ananizapta), giving one word for each of its letters. The second addition is on rhetoric. Alan's text begins anew on the following page, , preceded by a prologue beginning: Incipiunt prouerbia alani ad instructionem morum.
On two excerpts from Jerome and Johannes Chrysostomus have been added.

msItem.colophon: Finitus per me Cristianum Hymmendorpen in Cellula claustrali monasterii sancti Michaelis in luneborge sub anno domini 1474 Die sanctorum apostolorum philippi et iacobi

msItem.title: Excerpts from the Bible, the Church Fathers, verses, and texts on the liturgy and the mass
msItem.note:
This section contains excerpts, verses and small treatises written in by many different hands. Among them are: : Machmet prophanus decepit arte paganos / Talmod iudeos wicklef anglicos hussoque bohemos; : Roma potens reuerenda rauenna, Walther (1959)
; : Notandum vina probantur odore colore et sapore unde / Si bona vina cupis hec tria probatur in illis / Forcia formosa fragrancia frigida nec non / Item bonum vinum meliores gignit humores / Gignit humores melius vinum meliores Sunt nutritiua plus dulcia candida vina / Et debet esse clarum / Si fuerit nigrum corpus confert tibi pigrum / Item vina simul cum lacte non debent sumi / Si sumis vina simul et lac sit tibi lepra / Vinum et mulieres apostotare faciunt sapientes; : Verus amor numquam quiescit semper agit et numquam tepescit; Officium misse valet obsequium super omne, Walther (1959)
. : A collection on verses and texts mainly on the liturgy and the mass, the first beginning: Collecta dicta misse sit pars ibi prima / Cristi prima dies pars designat pueriles / Pars euangelio dinoscitur altera lecto.

msItem.title: Excerpt from Bonaventure's Soliloquium de quatuor mentalibus excercitiis
msItem.incipit: Estimo quod mundanum gaudium
msItem.explicit: florent falsis bonis pereunt veris tormentis augustinus
msItem.note: This is an excerpt from Ch. 2, 9 of the Soliloquium. The last four lines, marked off from the first part by a row of red xs, are from Ch. 2, 6. Below the text on additions by two contemporary hands.

msItem.title: De regimine vitae
msItem.rubric: Doctrina sancti bernhardi de regimine vite
msItem.incipit: Si vis ducere bonam vitam hec regulariter et firmiter teneas
msItem.explicit: Time deum sequere eum Non propria voluntatem habeas Sepe confitearis

note.p: Below the verses an addition by a different hand of three lines in prose.

msItem.title: Opus tripartitum (De praeceptis decalogi, De confessione, De arte moriendi)
msItem.rubric: INcipit opusculum tripartitum de preceptis decalogi de confessione et de arte morigendi per eximium sacre theoloye professorem magistrum iohannem yerson alme vniuersitatis parisiensis cancellarium et cetera
msItem.incipit: CRistianitati suisque suus qualiscumque zelatoribus prosperum ad uirtutes
msItem.explicit: ad hoc ipsum deputato paratus confiteri se continuo presentaret sicut in domo dei parisiensi laudabiliter obseruatur
msItem.finalRubric: Explicit opusculum tripartitum de preceptis decalogi de confessione et de arte moriendi Editum per eximium sacre theologie professorem Magistrum iohannem gerson Alme vniuersitatis parisiensis cancellarium
msItem.note:
Listed in Bloomfield et al. (1979)
.
msItem.colophon: Scriptum anno domini mo cccco lxxvo In vigilia annunctiacionis gloriosissime virginis Marie et cetera

msItem.title: De modo vivendi
msItem.rubric: Incipit tractatus bonus eiusdem de modo viuendi omnium fidelium Ad nobiles
msItem.incipit: REgula prima que spectat ad nobiles potentes et scabinos
msItem.explicit: Qui hanc regulam secuti fuerint Pax super illos et misericordia Amen
msItem.finalRubric: Expliciunt regule puch pulchre de modo viuendi omnium fidelium Edite per eximium sacre theoloye professorem magistrum iohannem gerson Alme vniuersitatis parisiensis cancellarium Scriptum anno et die ut supra et cetera deo gracias
msItem.note:
Listed in Bloomfield et al. (1979)
.

msItem.title: Admonitio morienti et de peccatis suis nimium formidanti
msItem.incipit: NOta uerba anscelmi quomodo informandus sit aliquis in extremis frater proximus morti sic interrogetur Frater letaris quod in fide christiana morieris
msItem.explicit: In manus tuas domine commendo spiritum meum Securus moritur cui hec ante mortem dicuntur nec videbit mortem eternam Hec in scriptis beati anscelmi inueniuntur

note.p:
On a verse has been added: Sunt carnis famule tres lubrica lingua soluti / Renes ingluuies ambiciosa gule / Hec tria displiceant lasciua senecta coacta / Latria pauperies deliciosa petens / Afflictu triplici nos ledunt tres inimici / Serpens vulnificus caro lubrica mundus iniquus / Est ancilla dei simplex elemosina mortis / Antidotum venie porta salutis iter / Est dare leticie vox verbum nobile verbum / mellifluum dantis gloria dantis honor / Est rogo vox miseri vox importuna pudoris / prodiga meroris plena fauoris egens (cf. Werner
).

Below the verse a coloured drawing of two people kissing with text in red from Cant. 1:1: Osculetur me osculoris sui canticorum io . The drawing points to the text beginning on , postils on the Song of Songs.

msItem.title: Postils on the Song of Songs
msItem.rubric: Incipiunt cantica canticorum Salomonis literaliter Osculetur me osculo oris sui quia meliora sunt vbera tua vino fragrancia vngentis optimis
msItem.incipit: Ad intellectum istius libri qui cantica canticorum nuncupatur quantum ad sensum literalem est sciendum Quod iste rex salomon
msItem.explicit: quia sum victa per te nam dilectio tua est sincerissima quare non valeo tibi in hoc coequare
msItem.note: Below the colophon on and continuing on a number of sayings and verses are added, the first five: Malam uxorem pocius risu quam baculo castigabis / Famulum suis moribus blandientem repelle / Famulum se verecundantem de facili dilige ut filium / Si vis edificare domum inducat te necessitas et non voluptas / Melius est grauem pati famem quam patrimonii vendicionem sed melius est vendere quam se vsuris subicere.
msItem.colophon: Anno et cetera lxxviio in vigilia natiuitatis marie virginis materia hec est completa per me iohannem himendorpp

msItem.title: In dedicatione altaris
msItem.incipit: Edificauit noe altare domino et tollens de cunctis pecoribus et volucribus mundis Obtulit holocaustum super altare Gen viiio Hoc factum est quando egrediebatur de archa post diluuium
msItem.explicit: manus enim vestre plene sunt sanguine (Isa. 1:15) id est operaciones vestre plene sunt peccatis et ideo frustra offert et cetera

msItem.title: Ars memorativa (Memoria fecunda)
msItem.incipit: MEmoria fecunda deus pater eternus generatiua intellectualis filii verbi sapiencie coeterne
msItem.explicit: ut ea que in figuris memoriter ponimus sepe repetere debemus
msItem.note:
In the colophon the text is attributed to the otherwise unknown Johannes Wildruden. The text seems to be a commentary on or a rewrit of the anonymous Memoria fecunda from the early 15th century, the earliest extant version of which is edited by Pack (1979)
. A brief text on the miracles of Christ is added after the text on .
msItem.colophon: Anno domini incarnacionis lxxxviii in castro Wulff a venerabili viro domino Iohanni Wildruden ibidem protunc diuinorum rectori collectum de quo laudetur deus et pia mater eius

msItem.title: De arte memorativa
msItem.incipit: Memoria que secundum tulium inventorem Est thezaurus deo fauente tractaturus premittendum est
msItem.explicit: xxia doctrina consorcium malorum summe euitandum Deo gracias
msItem.note:
Further manuscripts transmitting this text is listed in Heimann-Seelbach (2000)
.
msItem.colophon: Completum ipso die mercurii que fuit sexta mensis Augusti Anno et cetera lxxxviiio in castro wulffesborg

msItem.title: In dedicatione ecclesiae
msItem.incipit: Querebat videre ihesum quis esset (Luke 19:3) luce xix pro nostra aliquali informacione et domus dei cuius memoria agitur
msItem.explicit: vocatur celsa ab altitudine et dicitur a sicos id est ficus et morum Inde sicomorum

msItem.title: In dedicatione ecclesiae
msItem.incipit: Beati qui habitant in domo tua (Ps. 83:5) Pensandum nobis est que sit domus domini
msItem.explicit: vbi terror animarum numquam lassatur nos protegere dignetur ihesus christus dominus noster qui est benedictus in secula seculorum Amen
msItem.note: A short sermon has been added after the text, beginning Vidi ciuitatem sanctam iherusalem nouam (Rev. 21:2).

msItem.title: De corpore Christi
msItem.incipit: Vidi in dextera sedentis ... (Rev. 5:1) Iste liber est corpus christi Sigilla vii in isto libro sunt vii miracula
msItem.explicit: Si ergo creatura non polluitur multo minus non polluitur creator et hoc multi dubitant et cetera
msItem.note: Below the text is an added line beginning: Item gestum id est factum.

msItem.title: In dedicacione templi
msItem.incipit: Vidi ciuitatem sanctam iherusalem nouam (Rev. 21:2) apocalipsis xxi Ista ciuitas anagoyce intelligens est superna et celestis patria
msItem.explicit: Totus mundus in maligno positus est et ergo ve ve ve habitantibus in terra (Rev. 8:13) Apocalipsis et cetera

msItem.title: De psalterio beatae virginis Mariae bulla; exempla et miracula
msItem.incipit: SIxtus episcopus Seruus seruorum dei Ad perpetuam rei memoriam ea que ex fidelium deuocione ad dei omnipotentis () Notandum sicud turpiter in gestis pontificum
msItem.explicit: propterea in corde dabo tibi hanc benedictionem vt sencias ibi distincte filii mei vitam

note.p: In the bottom margin on is written: hic est defectus ad 4or monilia subsequncia quia exemplar non habuit plus.

msItem.title: German texts on the Marian Psalter
A miracle connected to the Marian Psalter
msItem.rubric: Wii gudt dat is to bedende den salter der iuncfrouwen Marien mogen gy mercken in dussem nageschrevenn Mirakele
msItem.incipit: EYn karthuser der Aldererwerdigesten thelerynne goddes gancz ynnich vnd leeff was
msItem.explicit: in dem verteynhundersten () iare na der bordt christi Salich ys deienne de eynen andern alzo leret

msItem.title: A rosary miracle
msItem.rubric: Van dem rosenkranczse eyn Mercklich mirakell
msItem.incipit: EYn van den vedern de dar storuen Na der bordt christi verteynhunderth iar vnd eynvndedrittigh iar In der karthus by Treere
msItem.explicit: schrift sy gewesen Godt sy des gelouet Amen
msItem.note: The text is followed by a brief text in Latin with the heading MARIA and beginning Maria sic interpretatur. Four lines of verse in German in red ink follow: Ihesus maria vnd alle hymmelsche schaer / Vns moite geuen de ewigen salicheyt / Dat wy mogen alle komen daer / Dorgh ore vordenst vnd milden barmherticheyt Amen.

On , excerpts in Latin from Gregory, Jerome, Ambrose, Augustine, Isidore, Origen, Eusebius, Haymo, Cassian, and Hugh of St Victor are written within the text block, at the bottom of each page. On further excerpts from Jerome, Augustine, Anselm and Bernhard occupy most of the page.

msItem.title: Rosary clauses
msItem.rubric: Meditaciones circa psalterium et rosarium beate marie virginis gloriossisime pater noster
msItem.incipit: 1 Per angelum marie nunciatus
msItem.explicit: 10 Qui veniet singulos diiudicare

note.p: The clauses are followed by a short prayer: Sancte et indiuidue trinitati simplicissime vnitati vt mihi sit auxilium nunc et post hoc exilium Amen.

msItem.title: Excerpts from the Church Fathers and the Bible
msItem.note: Excerpts from Origen, Gregory, Augustine, Ps.-John Chrysostom, and Proverbs.

msItem.title: Instructions for mass
msItem.incipit: Sacerdos quilibet antequam missam domini ad celebrandum
msItem.explicit: Hanc figuram composuit Bonaventura ordinis fratrum minorum missam legenti et deuite considerati centum dies indulgenciarum

msItem.title: Two short texts
msItem.note: The first text concerns eligibilty to celebrate the eucharist; the second comments on John 16:20 drawing on excerpts from Thomas of Aquino and Gregory.

msItem.title: A collection of excerpts
msItem.rubric: Considera tu presbiter diligenter subsequencia collecta ex diuersis libris et materiis
msItem.incipit: Osee scriptum est peccata populi mei
msItem.explicit: quod ipsa sit in nobis possumus videre per noticiam intellectualem
msItem.note:
Listed in Bloomfield et al. (1979)
.

msItem.title: Epistola Luciferi
msItem.rubric: Incipit Epistola Luciferi principis demoniorum Ad potentes et Ad prelatos ad plebanos et curatos Ad episcopos et monachos mendicantes et taxillorum lusores
msItem.incipit: Lvcifer princeps tenebrarum tristicie profunda regens achirontis
msItem.explicit: In robur fidem et testimonium omnium et singulorum premissorum
msItem.note:
The epistula consists of eleven sections. A shorter variant is found in Uppsala, Universitetsbiblioteket, C 212, see Andersson-Schmitt, Margarete och Hedlund, Monica (1990)
, edited by Schade (1856)
.
msItem.colophon: Explicit epistola luciferi () principis demoniorum die Solis que fuit xxiiia mensis Augusti Anno domini et cetera 1489 In castro wuluessborgh

msItem.title: Speculum aureum decem praeceptorum (excerpts)
msItem.rubric: Circa decem precepta aliqua sunt prenotanda vt patebit per ordinem inferius
msItem.incipit: Audi israel et obserua vt facias que precepit tibi deus et bene sit tibi (Deut. 6:3) Spiritus sanctus per organum illud singulare moysen loquens hiis verbis
msItem.explicit: Vnde tota interior operacio dyaboli videtur esse circa fantasiam et appetitum sensitiuum
msItem.finalRubric: Et hec breuiter de x preceptis Ex preceptorio eximii doctoris herpp fratrum ordinis fratrum minorum collectis

msItem.title: Excerpts and short texts
msItem.note: Excerpts from the Church Fathers, the Bible, and other authorities on curates and priests, clerical concubinage, silence and speech, poverty, and the seven deadly sins.

p.note: Five different oxhead watermarks, all with eyes, ears, and single-line rod with a quatrefoil: (also with nostrils); ; ; (oxhead and snake (?)); .

supportDesc.condition: The first quire is almost detached from the binding. The fore-edge colouring has bled into some of the leaves, e.g. , .

handDesc.p: The manuscript is written in gothic cursive by several different hands. The identification of hands is tentative and lists only the hands responsible for larger sections of the manuscript.
handDesc.handNote: Gothic cursive. Possibly a new hand on .
Gothic cursive. The scribe is Christianus Hymmendorpen, see colophon on .
Gothic cursive. The scribe is Johannes Himendorpp, see colophon on .
Gothic cursive. Possibly a new hand on and on . The letter d is often written without a loop on the ascender.
Gothic cursive. The letter d is often written without a loop on the ascender.
Gothic cursive. The letter d is almost always written without a loop on the ascender.
Gothic cursive. The letter d is almost always written without a loop on the ascender.
Gothic cursive. The letter d is almost always written without a loop on the ascender.

p.note: There are textual additions by many different hands throughout the manuscript both in the form of briefer comments and keywords from the texts and longer additions in Latin and German, e.g. , and . Some additions are described in a note under Contents.
In the upper margin of is written: Fasciculus Antiquus by an early modern hand, followed by a crossed-out word and an illegible word.
A coloured drawing of a man and a woman kissing. Red text from Cant. 1:1 added next to it: Osculetur me osculoris sui canticorum io .
Two manicules in red in the outer margin.

decoDesc.p: Text in black and brown ink. Capitals touched in red. Some rubrics and underlining in red.

decoNote.p: : Plain initials in red over 1-4 lines, a few with interior reserved shapes, e.g. and .
: Plain initials in red over 4-5 lines with interior reserved shapes.
Edges coloured in blue-green but the colour has mostly worn off.
